title: nassella entrerriensis
intro: Nassella entrerriensis, also known as Argentine needle grass, is a perennial grass native to South America. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Nassella entrerriensis include its ability to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate pain. In herbal preparations, Nassella entrerriensis is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
